item.title: A modular skill and orchestration framework for traceable agentic computational chemistry
item.description: Large-language-model agents can now operate the software of computational chemistry, but how their science compares with the answers human experts produced remains untested. Here we present AI Computational Chemist (AICC), a modular skill framework that ...

item.title: Understanding and Reporting PFAS Exposure: Wide-scope Retrospective Suspect Screening of High-resolution Mass Spectrometry Data from Exposomics Studies
item.description: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) comprise thousands of persistent and structurally diverse chemicals that contribute to chronic human exposure. Yet, routine biomonitoring targets only a limited set of regulated legacy compounds, underestimating ...

item.title: Antibacterial Activity of Metal-Free Bipyridines: Vinylpyridine versus Methyl Substituents
item.description: In this work, we report that incorporating a vinylpyridine arm into 4,4′-dimethyl-2,2′-bipyridine (L1) significantly enhances antibacterial potency against both Gram-positive (B. subtilis,S. epidermidis,S. aureus) and Gram-negative (S. enterica,E. coli)...

item.title: β-Stereoselective Glycosylation without Acyl Participation: Evaluation of β-(1→2)-Linked Disaccharide Donors and Their Application to Glycoarray Design for Antibody Characterization
item.description: β-(1→2)-Glucans constitute a relatively rare and substantially underexplored class of polysaccharides. Of particular interest is the macrocyclic β-(1→2)-glucan from Brucella (CβG), which regarded as a promising diagnostic marker of brucellosis. However, ...

item.title: Molecular Motion-Activated Spin-Vibronic Coupling: A Pre-Twisting Design Strategy for Efficient Pure Organic Room Temperature Phosphorescence
item.description: Suppressing molecular motion in the solid state has long been regarded as a golden key for achieving efficient room temperature phosphorescence (RTP), as it minimizes the rates of nonradiative decay and external quenching. Here we demonstrate a ...

item.title: Inverse theoretical spectroscopy via Monte Carlo sampling: From spectra to ensembles of molecular structures
item.description: A molecule’s structure determines its spectroscopic properties, and vice versa, molecular spectra are indispensable for elucidating its three-dimensional structure. Here, we present a Monte Carlo sampling scheme for generating an ensemble of molecular ...

item.title: Atomically precise metal nanoclusters in nitrate-to-ammonia electrocatalysis
item.description: Driven by the growing need for sustainable ammonia production and effective nitrate remediation, the electrocatalytic nitrate reduction reaction (NO3RR) has emerged as a promising technology. This process enables the conversion of nitrate pollutants into ...

item.title: Proteomics as an Emerging Tool for Understanding Biotransformation and Toxicological Impacts of Contaminants
item.description: Mass-spectrometry-based proteomics is rapidly advancing, becoming fast, affordable, and increasingly accessible to environmental scientists. This perspective outlines how proteomics can illuminate biotransformation processes and sublethal organism-level ...

item.title: Switching Reactivity with Light: C–P Bonding Controls Singlet Oxygen Generation and Reactivity in DHP–CPD Photoswitches
item.description: The dimethyldihydropyrene photochromic system is a 14π-electronic framework that switches to a lessaromatic non-planar system with visible light. In this work we unravel a neutral phosphorus heteroatom-appended dimethyldihydropyrene (DHP) photoswitchable ...

item.title: Metagenomics and Machine Learning for Per-and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS): A Review of Convergent Approaches, 2020–2025
item.description: Per-and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are persistent, mobile, and toxic contaminants whose carbon–fluorine bonds resist conventional remediation. Two computational-experimental fields have grown rapidly and begun to converge on the problem: ...

item.title: RESEARCH ARTICLE Fast Isotopic [18F]Fluoride Exchange in Iminosulfur Oxydifluorides and Triflimidoyl Fluorides: A novel approach for [18F]tagging of primary amine-derived precursors
item.description: Direct incorporation of fluorine-18 into primary amine- derived molecules remains a major challenge in radiochemistry. Current strategies largely rely on multistep conjugation of electrophilic prosthetic groups, limiting automation and generating ...

item.title: A Novel Approach for Monitoring The Spatial Distribution and Quantitative Analysis of Micronutrients in Plant Tissues Using Laser Ablation Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry Imaging
item.description: Quantitative imaging of plant tissues by laser ablation inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (LA-ICP-MS) is hindered by the lack of matrix-matched calibration standards. Established approaches, such as gelatine or homogenized tissue blocks, do not ...

item.title: Spin Magnetoreception Through Redox-Active Residues and Nucleotides
item.description: The radical pair mechanism explains how low-intensity magnetic fields influence the coherent singlet–triplet interconversion of spin-correlated radical pairs formed by electron transfer in photoredox-active biological chromophores. Although this mechanism ...

item.title: MARS-RNA: An Automated Pipeline for Correlating RNA Molecular Dynamics Trajectories with Chemical Probing Data
item.description: Ribonucleic acid (RNA) molecules fold into complex, highly dynamic three-dimensional conformational structures that govern their biological activities. Chemical probing methods, such as SHAPE (selective 2’-hydroxyl acylation analyzed by primer extension), ...

item.title: Excited state dynamics of an air-stable low-spin carbene manganese(III) dual charge transfer chromophore
item.description: We report synthesis, structure and properties of a N-heterocyclic carbene pyridine manganese(III) complex featuring a low-spin ground state. The complex exhibits excellent stability under ambient conditions in the solid state and even dissolved in water. ...

item.title: Systematic Investigation of Reaction Parameters and Purification Strategies for DNA-Polymer Conjugates
item.description: DNA-polymer conjugates are an emerging class of biohybrid materials with applications in therapeutics and biosensing, yet quantitative guidelines for optimizing conjugation conversion and purification remain unreported. Here, we systematically investigate ...

item.title: Synthetic Pathway Modulation of Metals Ordering in Multivariate Metal-Organic Frameworks
Spin-Resolved Dielectric Bath Embedding Theory for Metallic Surfaces
item.description: We introduce spin-resolved dielectric bath embedding theory (SR-DBET), which incorporates a physics-informed embedding potential to describe attractive and repulsive interactions, a charge dielectric bath to capture charge polarization, and a spin ...

item.title: VPT2 Meets MLPs: IR and Raman Spectroscopy of Eight Molecules, Including 74-Atom Cholesterol, Using MACE-OFF and MACE-MDP
item.description: Vibrational second order perturbation theory is a widely used method to perform post-harmonic calculations of vibrational energies and infrared (IR) and Raman spectroscopy. We recently reported an application of this approach for vibrational energies ...

item.title: A Wearable Multimodal Platform for Monitoring Plant Heat Stress via Leaf VOCs and Relative Humidity
item.description: Heat stress limits plant productivity by disrupting transpiration, altering leaf microclimate, and activating metabolic pathways that increase volatile organic compound (VOC) emissions. VOC signatures, combined with leaf-level relative humidity (RH), ...

item.title: The effect of Pt concentration on lean methane oxidation over Pd-Pt/CeO2 catalysts depends on ceria faceting
item.description: Pd:Pt/CeO2catalysts supported on shape-controlled CeO2nanorods and nanocubes were investigated for lean methane oxidation to determine how Pt substitution and support morphology affect activity, stability, water inhibition, and redox behavior. Nanocube-...

item.title: Ruthenium-Catalyzed N-Dealkylation of Aromatic Amines
item.description: In industrial organic chemistry, amines and N-alkylamines represent crucial intermediates. Conversely, N-methyl amines are undesirable byproducts of recycling processes of polyamides or polyurethanes that involve methanolysis or hydrogenolysis steps. ...

item.title: CACHE Challenge #4: Targeting the TKB Domain of the E3 ligase CBLB, an Immuno-Oncology Target
item.description: The Critical Assessments of Computational Hit-finding Experiments (CACHE) are prospective benchmarking exercises where small molecule ligands predicted for a nominated protein of interest are procured and tested experimentally. In CACHE #4, 23 ...

item.title: Single-Molecule Fluorescence Reveals Catalytic Heterogeneity and Active Species in Copper Nanocatalyzed Click Reactions
item.description: The copper-catalyzed azide–alkyne cycloaddition (CuAAC) is a cornerstone of click chemistry and has been widely employed in many applications. However, its mechanism with heterogeneous catalysts remains incompletely understood. Catalytic reactions ...

item.title: Early Detection and Recovery of SCF Convergence Failures in Automated Quantum Chemistry Workflows via Time-Series Learning
item.description: Self-consistent field (SCF) convergence failures represent a major bottleneck in high-throughput workflows, particularly for open-shell systems and material discovery. To address this challenge, we introduce an autonomous pipeline that treats the ...

item.title: Programming Electrocatalytic Alkene Epoxidation via Potentiodynamic Control of Surface Oxygen
item.description: Electrocatalytic alkene epoxidation offers an attractive route to epoxides using water as an oxygen source at near ambient conditions, but direct electrochemical strategies often suffer from low selectivity and limited catalytic activity. Here, we ...

item.title: Infrared analysis suitable for high-throughput detection of keratin film formation from sheep wool material
item.description: Sheep wool represents an abundant and underutilized bioresource, primarily composed of keratin, with significant potential for the development of sustainable biomaterials. In this study, the formation of keratin films from wool was investigated using a ...

item.description: Computational kinetic modeling relies on accurate transition-state geometries and activation barriers. While machine learning models trained on ab initio data have shown promise for transition-state search and barrier prediction, they often struggle to ...

item.title: Spin Manifolds in the [Fe4S4(SCH3)4]2− Cluster from Localized Active Space State Interaction Singles and Pair-Density Functional Theory

item.description: In the widely-used Kohn-Sham density functional theory for the many-electron ground state, only the density functional for the exchange-correlation energy is approximated. While the exact density functional minimizes at the exact ground-state energy for ...

item.description: The application of large language models (LLMs) to scientific research has raised growing expectations for autonomous discovery. Although LLMs are capable of scientific reasoning, literature investigation and hypothesis generation, they lack feedback ...

item.description: Electroosmotic transport through branching microchannel networks plays a central role in numerous charged biological porous media, membranes, and microscale physiological systems, yet existing optimization theories are largely restricted to uniformly ...

item.title: Synthesis, Redox Properties and Magneto-Optical Activity of a Homologous Set of Metallo-5,5-Dimethylisocorrole Complexes

item.description: Bio-oil derived from lignocellulosic biomass represents a sustainable alternative to fossil fuels as an energy source and chemical feedstock. Fast pyrolysis of lignocellulosic biomass yields a rich mixture of oxygenated organic species with low energy ...

item.title: Competing Substrate and Free-Surface Effects on Ion Transport in Ultrathin Polymer Electrolyte Films

item.description: The commitment of a reacting system to productive barrier crossing depends on how strongly the environment dissipates energy into the approach-region coordinate, rather than oscillatory recrossing. This dissipation is quantified by a single dimensionless ...

item.description: Electrochemical ethylene oxidation can produce ethylene oxide and ethylene glycol, but selectivity is limited by competition with the oxygen evolution reaction and overoxidation. At the same time, the mechanistic literature is fragmented across oxo-, ...

item.description: Cell culture platforms benefit from chemical cues that recapitulate key aspects of the native biological environment. However, substantial challenges remain in designing scaffolds that also control the spatial organization of these cues. This is ...

item.description: The application of an external electric field is emerging as a promising alternative to laborious structural modifications to promote catalytic reactions. While theoretical models predict that electric fields can substantially influence the critical ...

item.title: In Silico Prediction and Optimization of a potential Therapeutic Peptide Targeting the Nipah Virus G Glycoprotein
item.description: The highly fatal Nipah virus requires the development of effective antiviral therapeutics. Viral attachment is initiated by the interaction of the G glycoprotein with the host Ephrin-B2 and Ephrin-B3 receptors, making this protein an attractive ...

item.title: Light drives bidirectional two-electron transfer between paired dynamic redox molecules
item.description: We demonstrate closed photoredox systems in which light alone drives a net two-electron transfer between pairs of dynamic redox (dyrex) switches. The same pair can be cycled between two thermodynamically stable states using light of orthogonal wavelengths,...

item.title: Learning slow manifolds from time order
item.description: Time series are ubiquitous, from climate science, healthcare to economics. The high dimensionality, complex correlations, and multiscale dynamics in rich time-series data challenge expert intuition and drive the development of machine-learning approaches. ...

item.title: Virtual Screening and In Silico Evaluation of Antidepressants as Novel MexB Efflux Pump Inhibitors in Pseudomonas aeruginosa

item.description: Copolymerizations of two or more monomers are important, because they allow for tailoring the polymer properties by the selection of monomers and by the amount of each monomer in the product. While tailoring of the copolymer composition is relatively easy ...

item.description: Heterogeneous catalysts under reaction conditions have crowded surfaces where lateral interactions dictate reaction energetics. Understanding and predicting these interactions, however, remains a formidable challenge. First-principles calculations suffer ...

item.title: Biocatalytic, Enantioselective Oxa-Pictet-Spengler Reactions for the Asymmetric Synthesis of Tetrahydropyranoindoles
item.description: We report that ZmoSHC1 exhibits new-to-nature activity for oxa-Pictet-Spengler reactions using tryptophol vinyl ethers as substrates. Directed evolution is used to engineer a single mutant, ZmoSHC1 Y702L, which demonstrates high activity (up to 3370 TTN) ...

item.title: A Density Matrix Theory for Photothermal Two-Dimensional Infrared Spectroscopy
item.description: Two-dimensional infrared (2D-IR) spectroscopy can resolve vibrational anharmonicity, mode coupling, and energy transfer. The conventional 2D-IR detects nonlinear polarization that generates propagating photons after sample’s light matter interaction with ...

item.title: Bismuth-Catalyzed Carbonylation of C-N Bonds in Tertiary Aniline Derivatives
item.description: A novel catalytic system is presented that uses readily producible or commercially available bismuth iodides as catalysts to carbonylate tertiary aniline derivatives into their corresponding tertiary amides. This challenging reaction demonstrates the ...

item.title: Cristobalite-Type Compounds for Rechargeable Potassium-Ion Batteries
item.description: Potassium-ion batteries have emerged as high-voltage and economically advantageous electrochemical energy storage systems with promise to viably complement and/or augment lithium-ion technology owing to the natural abundance, projected low cost and ...

item.title: 25 Years of Cardiac Ion Channel QSAR: From hERG Dominance to Multi-Channel Modelling
item.description: In 2013 the Comprehensive in vitro Proarrhythmia Assay (CiPA) initiative proposed multi-channel assessment for proarrhythmic risk evaluation, yet ligand-based predictive modelling has remained largely single-channel. To quantify and explain this mismatch, ...

item.title: Femto- to Microsecond Excited-State Dynamics of Aggregated and Monomeric Regiopure Zinc and Copper Phthalocyanines
item.description: Understanding how aggregation state and metal identity affect excited-state dynamics of phthalocyanines (Pcs) is essential for rationalising their performance as photosensitisers in phototherapeutic applications. Here, we investigate the steady-state and ...

item.title: Armstrong's Range Exponent Electron Configuration Model (AREECM)
item.description: Traditional electron-configuration notation (e.g., 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6) represents multi-electron systems using integer orbital occupations that reflect dominant configurations but do not explicitly encode the multi-configurational nature of correlated ...

item.title: KinSPEC: A Kinase Sequence-Based Prior-Guided Framework for Predicting Compound-Protein Interactions
item.description: In silico virtual screening has become an important strategy in early-stage drug discovery for identifying compound-protein interactions (CPIs). Structure-based and ligand-based approaches are widely used but rely heavily on the availability of high-...

item.title: Conformation-Guided Disulfide Pairing Enables Efficient Folding of Disulfide-Rich Peptides
item.description: Disulfide bond formation is critical for the structural integrity and biological functions of peptides and proteins. For disulfide-rich peptides (DRPs), particularly those containing ≥ 3 disulfide bonds, this process remains challenging due to misfolding ...

item.title: Cobaloxime-Based Metalloradical Catalysis for C(sp3)–Si bond formation via Carbene Insertion into Hydrosilanes
item.description: Organosilicon compounds serve as valuable scaffolds with extensive applications across organic synthesis, materials chemistry, and pharmaceuticals. We demonstrate that cobaloximes effectively catalyze carbene radical insertion into Si–H bonds, utilizing α-...

item.title: Unveiling Nb2O5 as a Catalytically Functional Oxide for Thermocatalytic CO2 Hydrogenation
item.description: The thermocatalytic hydrogenation of CO2 typically relies on reducible oxides such as CeO2, TiO2, ZrO2 and In2O3 to achieve CO2 adsorption and activation, especially the cleavage of C=O bonds. Here, we report that niobium pentoxide (Nb2O5), when ...

item.title: Integrating Molecular Docking into Environmental Bioremediation Research: A Study of Fungal Enzyme–Pesticide Interactions
item.description: The extensive use of pesticides in agriculture has resulted in widespread environmental contamination, threatening ecosystems and human health. Bioremediation using fungal oxidative enzymes represents an eco-friendly approach for pesticide detoxification. ...

item.title: Competitive Coordination Reveals Organofluoride-Stabilized SEI in All-Solid-State Lithium Metal Batteries with Polymer Electrolyte
item.description: Solid polymer electrolytes (SPEs) suffer from limited lithium (Li) ion migration kinetics due to strong solvation between Li ions and polymer chains, as well as severe Li dendrite growth driven by unstable solid electrolyte interphase (SEI), whose dynamic ...

item.title: Interfacial structure of conjugated polymer photocatalysts with water and scavengers for sacrificial hydrogen evolution
item.description: Conjugated polymers have emerged as competitive photocatalysts for the sustainable production of hydrogen from water. Their synthetic versatility allows for systematic tuning of their opto-electronic properties and polarity to improve visible-light-driven ...

item.title: Switching Reactivity with Light: C–P Bonding Controls Singlet Oxygen Generation and Reactivity in DHP–CPD Photoswitches
item.description: The dimethyldihydropyrene photochromic system is a 14π-electronic framework that switches to a lessaromatic non-planar system with visible light. In this work we unravel a neutral phosphorus heteroatom-appended dimethyldihydropyrene (DHP) photoswitchable ...

item.title: From zero-dimensional square tetramers to one-dimensional screw chains: a structural transformation in V3+-based lead vanadium fluorides
item.description: Low-dimensional magnetic materials have attracted considerable research attention in materials science and condensed matter physics due to the emergence of exotic phenomena induced by their strong quantum fluctuation. Herein, two low-dimensional lead ...

item.title: Upcycling CO2 and Forest-Coastal Biomass into Mineral-Organic Nanocomposite Additives for Biobased Skincare and Protective Surface Coatings
item.description: The integration of CO2 capture with residual biomass valorization offers a promising route to sustainable, multifunctional materials for cosmetic and surface protection applications. In this work, biobased nanocomposites were developed by combining two ...

item.title: THERE IS NO KNEE IN RAGONE: CLARIFYING BATTERY ENERGY AND POWER FADE USING RAGONE PLOTS
item.description: Ragone plots provide a compact representation of energy storage performance by relating accessible energy to power. Despite this, battery degradation is often described using simplified metrics such as “energy fade” and “power fade”, which obscure changes ...

item.title: Robotic data management for trustworthy AI: A perspective from the perovskite semiconductor community
item.description: Autonomous laboratories are transforming materials research by combining robotic experimentation with artificial intelligence (AI) - guided discovery. As these platforms generate increasingly large and complex datasets, effective data management has ...

item.title: Coupled microkinetic and DFT calculations reveal that polaron-mediated oxygen transfer dictates lean methane oxidation on Pd/CeO2 catalysts
item.description: Lean CH4oxidation over Pd/CeO2catalysts is governed by metal-support redox interactions. Here, PdOx/CeO2−ycatalysts were synthesized with comparable PdOxparticle sizes but distinct ceria facet terminations, allowing support-morphology effects to be ...

item.description: Heterogeneous catalytic reactions in porous mixed-conducting solids enable energy conversion technologies from fuel cells to electrolyzers, yet the interplay between molecular-level rate-determining steps and their macroscopic spatial extent remains ...

item.title: Bismuth doping tailors the activity of Pd/CeO2 nanorods for lean methane oxidation
item.description: Lean methane oxidation remains challenging because PdOxactive sites are highly sensitive to the reaction environment, making support redox properties and Pd–ceria interfacial sites decisive. Here, bismuth was evaluated as a promoter for PdOx/CeO2−y...

item.title: Modeling Covalency with Molecular Analogues of Conductive Metal-Organic Frameworks
item.description: 2-D metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) with metallic or semiconductor charge mobilities continue to expand with ever increasing diversity in their molecular composition and hence orbital interactions. Predicting the charge transport behavior emerging from ...

item.title: Diamidophosphate-Mediated Formation of Organic Thiophosphorus Compounds from Thiols
item.description: Prebiotic environments on early Earth likely hosted diverse reactive networks in which sulfur and phosphorus chemistry intersected, providing plausible settings for the formation of organic thiophosphorus compounds featuring direct sulfur-phosphorus ...

item.description: Molecular Dynamics simulations are a valuable tool for investigating a wide variety of phenomena at the atomistic scale. However, it is this very high resolution that limits the time scales accessible to such simulations. Typically, this difficulty is ...

item.title: Point Defect analysis of chalcopyrite (CuFeS2) using First-principles calculation
item.description: Chalcopyrite (CuFeS2) have been investigated as a promising thermoelectric materials. In this study, native point defects of CuFeS2, which would affect the semiconductor properties, are investigated using first-principles calculations. The point defect ...

item.description: In recent years, quantum mechanics (QM)-based methods have offered superior accuracy for drug discovery, but their high computational costs have limited their application to the late stages of molecular optimization. To overcome this efficiency bottleneck ...

item.title: Mechanistic Insights into Nickel-Catalyzed Heck–Matsuda Reactions: Theoretical Evidence for a Ni(I)/Ni(III) Catalytic Cycle

item.title: Enhancing the robustness of rhodium single-atom catalysts for styrene hydroformylation through carbon nitride doping
item.description: Rh single-atom catalysts (SACs) have recently attracted significant attention as highly active heterogeneous catalysts for the hydroformylation of olefins. Despite the potential of these systems to become an alternative to the commercial and highly ...

item.title: Acoustic-Pressure-Guided Coalescence Enables Contactless Reagent Addition and 12-Hour Acoustic Levitation for Container-Free Proteomics
item.description: With advances in analytical sensitivity, low-input samples increasingly require sample-preparation workflows that limit analyte loss during multistep processing. Acoustic levitation (AL) provides a container-free aqueous reaction format, but its broader ...

item.description: Biotransformation alters the bioactivity of xenobiotics in humans; however, traditional approaches are infeasible for testing large numbers of metabolites. Herein, we propose a high-throughput CoSyER:ASMS approach for assessing the receptor-binding ...

item.title: Protodeboronation Enables the Formation of Dimethylsulfonium Cysteine for Controlled One-Pot Peptide and Protein Modification
item.description: The transformation of cysteine residues into more reactive species is a powerful entry point to post-translational protein modification and the synthesis of complex bioactive peptides, yet existing approaches often suffer from limited selectivity and ...

item.title: Revealing Hidden Organic Radicals in Carbon Black-hilled Rubber by Multi-harmonic EPR Spectroscopy
item.description: Carbon-black filled rubbers are widely used materials, yet the radical processes occurring within the elastomer matrix, particularly during vulcanization, remain poorly understood. Electron Paramagnetic Resonance (EPR) spectroscopy could provide direct ...

item.title: Characterizing the speciation and oxidation state of SnF2 in model toothpastes by 31P, 117/119Sn and 17O solution and solid-state NMR spectroscopy
item.description: Stannous fluoride (SnF2) is widely used in commercial toothpaste products as an anticaries fluoride source, antimicrobial agent, and desensitizer. However, oxidation of stannous ions (Sn2+) to stannic ions (Sn4+) results in lowered efficacy. To better ...

item.title: Multidimensional Temperature-Accelerated String Method: Sampling Multiple Transition Pathways and Slow Variables
item.description: The finite-temperature string (FTS) method identifies transition pathways between known metastable states in a selected collective-variable (CV) space, thereby reducing computational cost by focusing sampling along the pathway. Biomolecular transitions, ...

item.title: Quantum Computing and Artificial Neural Network methods in approximating the Quantum Many-Body Fermion System for Modern Quantum Chemistry Applications
item.description: The field of quantum chemistry has long been defined by the central computational challenge of the quantum many-body fermionic system problem, main the electronic Schrödinger equation. This exponential scaling makes it computationally intractable to solve,...

item.title: Carbothermal Reduction Reaction as a Strategy for Boosting Thermoelectric Performance of Binary Metal Oxides: A Case Study of ZnO Ceramics
item.description: As a powerful concept, grain boundary engineering has been increasingly applied to enhance energy conversion efficiency in thermoelectric (TE) materials through directly modulating electronic and thermal characteristics. However, most efforts so far have ...

item.title: Can catalytic activity be varied on different Cu sites for HCOO hydrogenation?
item.description: HCOO pathway dominates the production of CH3OH synthesis on Cu catalysts. HCOO hydrogenation is therefore of crucial importance for its kinetic hurdle. It is reported catalytic variation on different Cu facets as their surface Cu atoms are of different ...

item.title: Molecular Dynamics Study of Ions at Surface Nanobubble Interface: Adsorption Without Gas Retention
item.description: Surface nanobubbles are nanoscale gaseous domains anchored at solid–liquid interfaces, notable for their unexpected stability against dissolution. Although they share size and composition with bulk nanobubbles, the mechanisms underlying their persistence ...

item.title: Time-Resolved Raman Spectroscopy of Nitrate Electroreduction Reveals Dynamic Interfacial Adsorbate Evolution
item.description: Nitrate electroreduction offers a pathway for coupling wastewater remediation with sustainable ammonia production, yet its mechanistic details under operating conditions remain poorly understood. In this work, we directly resolve the dynamic evolution of ...

item.title: A Critical Evaluation of Domino and Cascade Reactions in Sustainable Organic Synthesis: Activation Modes and Green Metrics
item.description: While domino and cascade reactions are widely celebrated for their step economy and intrinsic bond-forming efficiency, evaluating their true sustainability requires moving beyond a purely molecular perspective to incorporate rigorous Green Chemistry ...

item.title: Pressure-Drop-Mediated Oscillatory Dynamics in Advanced Two-Phase Natural Circulation Systems: A Spectral Analytical Framework
item.description: This study presents a comprehensive linear stability analysis of a two-phase natural circulation loop (TPNCL), focusing on the prediction of instability regimes through analytical modeling. The system under consideration consists of interconnected single-...

item.description: Reaction kinetics is a foundational topic in undergraduate curricula. There is continued interest in developing instructional laboratory experiments that enable students to directly visualize reaction progress and to study reaction kinetics quantitatively ...

item.title: Can catalytic activity be varied on different Cu sites for HCOO hydrogenation?
item.description: HCOO pathway dominates the production of CH3OH synthesis on Cu catalysts. HCOO hydrogenation is therefore of crucial importance for its kinetic hurdle. It is reported catalytic variation on different Cu facets as their surface Cu atoms are of different ...

item.title: FAVES V4: A Production-Deployed Compliance and Liability Screening System for Drug Discovery at 122M-Compound Scale, Aligned to HHS/ONC FAVES and NIST AI 600-1
item.description: FAVES V4 is a compliance and liability screening system for drug discovery, aligned to two complementary federal frameworks: the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / Office of the National Coordinator FAVES rubric for trustworthy AI in ...

item.title: NovoExpert-2: State-of-the-Art ADMET Prediction via Gradient-Boosted Trees on MapLight Fingerprints and GIN Embeddings
item.description: We present NovoExpert-2, a dual-method approach to molecular property prediction that achieves state-of-the-art performance on five endpoints of the Therapeutics Data Commons (TDC) ADMET benchmark. Our primary model combines MapLight-style fingerprint fea-...

item.title: From 2D Graphs to Sub-Unit pKa: Nine Iterations to Beat MolGpKa on Both Scaffolds and Sulfonamides
item.description: Accurate pKa prediction for drug-like molecules remains a significant gap between com- mercial tools (Epik ∼0.5 RMSE, MoKa ∼0.4 RMSE) and open-source methods, particularly for sulfonamides and heterocyclic N–H compounds prevalent in medicinal chemistry. ...

item.title: Molecular Targets Shape Reasoning Topology: Bidirectional Validation of Chemistry-Inspired Bond Classification for AI Reasoning Chains
item.description: Recent work classified AI reasoning chains using chemistry-inspired bond types, namely covalent (deductive), hydrogen (reflective), and van der Waals (exploratory), showing that structural stability of reasoning predicts training data quality (Direction A)...

item.title: Human NEU3 and NEU4 enzymes prefer short polysialic acid substrates
item.description: Regulation of sialic acids is important to many biological processes and involves both biosynthesis and catabolism. The human neuraminidase enzymes (hNEU) regulate many sialosides in human health and disease. Polysialic acid (polySia) is a polyanion of α(...

item.title: A Machine Learning QSAR Model for Predicting hTERT Telomerase Inhibitor Potency: ChEMBL Dataset Curation, Algorithm Benchmarking, and Prospective Application to Natural and Synthetic Anticancer Compounds
item.description: Human telomerase reverse transcriptase (hTERT) is reactivated in approximately 85–90% of human cancers, establishing it as one of the most broadly expressed oncological drug targets; yet no quantitative structure–activity relationship (QSAR) model trained ...
